data.title: Rekor Systems Receives Nasdaq Notice for Bid Non-Compliance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Rekor Systems ( (REKR) ) is now available.

On April 27, 2026, Rekor Systems, Inc. disclosed that it had received a notice from Nasdaq indicating non-compliance with the exchange’s $1.00 minimum bid price requirement, after the company’s shares traded below that threshold for 30 consecutive business days from March 13 through April 24, 2026. The notice does not immediately affect trading, and Rekor’s stock will continue to trade on The Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “REKR.”

Rekor has been given until October 26, 2026, to regain compliance by sustaining a closing bid price of at least $1.00 for a minimum of ten consecutive business days, and it may use measures such as a reverse stock split to achieve this. If the company fails to meet the requirement by that date but satisfies other Nasdaq listing standards, it could receive an additional 180-day grace period, though persistent non-compliance could ultimately lead to delisting proceedings, introducing uncertainty for shareholders and potentially pressuring management to take corrective corporate actions.

The most recent analyst rating on (REKR) stock is a Buy with a $3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Rekor Systems stock, see the REKR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on REKR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, REKR is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (persistent losses and significant cash burn) and bearish technicals (below key moving averages with negative MACD). Offsetting this, earnings-call commentary indicates improving efficiency, margins, and backlog/visibility, while valuation and recent corporate actions are neutral to mildly supportive but not decisive.

To see Spark’s full report on REKR stock, click here.

More about Rekor Systems

Rekor Systems, Inc. operates in the technology sector and is listed on The Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker “REKR.” The company’s common stock is subject to Nasdaq’s continued listing standards, including minimum bid price requirements, which are critical for maintaining access to public equity markets and investor liquidity.

Average Trading Volume: 2,410,791

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $112M

data.title: Charles & Colvard Stalking Horse Bidder Approved in Bankruptcy
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Charles & Colvard ( (CTHRQ) ).

On March 2, 2026, Charles & Colvard filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Eastern District of North Carolina, initiating a court-supervised restructuring and sale process. The company entered into an asset purchase agreement with Van Lang Jewelry LLC or affiliate Jewelry Design Partners LLC for a $1.5 million transaction, under which the buyer would acquire substantially all assets, assume certain liabilities, and credit bid outstanding debtor-in-possession financing, with a final sale hearing set for June 22, 2026.

On April 29, 2026, the Bankruptcy Court approved the buyer as the stalking horse bidder, authorized its right to credit bid DIP obligations, approved related break-up fee and expense reimbursement, and cleared bidding procedures for potential competing offers. Separately, on April 27, 2026, the board shifted Executive Chair Michael Levin from a fixed term to a month-to-month appointment at $7,500 per month, and the company warned that common stockholders could face a significant or complete loss during the Chapter 11 case, making trading in its shares highly speculative and risky.

Spark’s Take on CTHRQ Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CTHRQ is a Neutral.

Charles & Colvard’s stock score is significantly impacted by its poor financial performance and valuation. The company’s strategic move to secure a convertible note agreement provides some positive outlook. However, technical indicators suggest a bearish trend, and the lack of earnings call data prevents a more comprehensive analysis.

To see Spark’s full report on CTHRQ stock, click here.

More about Charles & Colvard

Charles & Colvard, Ltd. is a North Carolina-based company in the jewelry industry, historically focused on designing and selling branded gemstone jewelry through wholesale and direct-to-consumer channels. The company’s operations and asset base are now subject to a Chapter 11 bankruptcy process, with its ongoing business potentially transitioning to new ownership through a court-supervised sale.

Average Trading Volume: 18,675

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $58.13K

On April 30, 2026, Kiniksa Pharmaceuticals announced that Chief Strategy Officer Eben Tessari had decided to resign effective May 15, 2026, to pursue another executive role in the life sciences industry, with the company stating his departure was not due to any disagreement over operations, policies, or practices. Upon leaving his executive post, Tessari will transition into a consulting role through May 15, 2027, advising on strategic and operational matters and serving as a special advisor to the Science and Research Committee, a move that helps preserve continuity in Kiniksa’s strategic direction while capping his compensated consulting work at 20 hours per month.

data.title: RB Global Shareholders Approve Board Slate and Governance Measures
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

RB Global ( (TSE:RBA) ) has shared an announcement.

At its April 30, 2026 annual and special meeting, RB Global shareholders approved setting the board size at ten directors and elected all ten nominees to one-year terms, reinforcing continuity in the company’s leadership and governance structure. Shareholders also reappointed Ernst & Young LLP as independent auditor and backed the non-binding “Say on Pay” executive compensation resolution, signaling overall support for current financial oversight and pay practices.

Investors further passed a special resolution giving the board flexibility to adjust the number of directors within the limits of the company’s governing documents, strengthening the directors’ ability to manage future board composition. A shareholder proposal to require all future shareholder meetings to be held in a hybrid in-person and virtual format was rejected, indicating the company and its investors prefer to retain discretion over how meetings are conducted.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:RBA) stock is a Buy with a C$130.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on RB Global stock, see the TSE:RBA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RBA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RBA is a Neutral.

The score is driven mainly by solid financial performance (scaled revenue base, positive earnings, and strong free cash flow) and supportive earnings-call guidance for continued GTV/EBITDA growth. These positives are tempered by weak technical momentum and a demanding valuation (high P/E), with rising leverage and margin compression as key fundamental watch items.

To see Spark’s full report on RBA stock, click here.

More about RB Global

RB Global, Inc. operates in the corporate and financial services sphere as a publicly traded company governed by a board of directors and overseen by independent auditors. Its shareholder base actively participates in annual and special meetings to elect directors, approve auditor appointments, and weigh in on executive compensation and governance matters.

data.title: Borealis Foods Secures New Oxus Loan, Reshapes Governance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Borealis Foods ( (BRLS) ).

On April 27, 2026, Borealis Foods’ subsidiaries entered into a $17 million secured term loan with major shareholder and former SPAC sponsor Oxus Capital, using the proceeds to fully repay about $16.2 million owed under a prior Frontwell Capital facility and related costs, freeing the company from Frontwell’s cash controls and associated restructuring requirements. The new loan, carrying 12% interest and a potential interest-for-equity conversion, also ties to a board shake-up adding two Oxus designees, while a separate conversion agreement could automatically turn roughly $33.3 million of shareholder debt into equity if a $70 million equity raise is not completed by July 1, 2026, materially diluting existing holders but easing going-concern pressures and reshaping governance and capital structure amid Nasdaq compliance challenges.

In parallel with the refinancing, Borealis terminated its Chief Restructuring Officer engagement, which had been imposed under the Frontwell forbearance, and secured board approval from disinterested directors for the related-party financing and conversion arrangements. These moves collectively provide longer-term liquidity, reduce immediate refinancing risk, and create a framework for substantial deleveraging via debt-to-equity conversions, but they also deepen Oxus’s influence and introduce potential dilution and control implications for current shareholders.

Spark’s Take on BRLS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BRLS is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by very weak financial performance (large losses, negative equity, and ongoing cash burn) and bearish technical signals (below major moving averages with negative MACD). Additional downside risk comes from recent governance and lender-default related corporate events, while valuation provides limited support due to ongoing losses and no dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on BRLS stock, click here.

More about Borealis Foods

Borealis Foods Inc., through subsidiaries including Palmetto Gourmet Foods, operates in the food manufacturing sector with production and distribution facilities in Saluda, South Carolina. The company has significant involvement from its former SPAC sponsor Oxus Capital PTE Ltd. and is listed on the Nasdaq Stock Market, where it has recently faced filing-related compliance issues.

Average Trading Volume: 1,684,636

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $35.63M

See more insights into BRLS stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Marpai Extends Maturity of Insider Promissory Notes
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Marpai ( (MRAI) ) has provided an update.

On February 13, 2026, Marpai, Inc. issued a $410,000 promissory note to Chief Executive Officer Damien Lamendola at a 12% annual interest rate, maturing April 11, 2026, and on March 9, 2026 it issued a second $250,000 note to him on the same terms maturing May 10, 2026. On April 29, 2026, Marpai and Lamendola agreed to extend the maturity dates of both notes, including all outstanding principal and interest, to September 1, 2026 while leaving all other terms unchanged, a move that eases near-term liquidity pressures and slightly lengthens the company’s short-term insider financing horizon.

The extension of these insider notes signals a continued willingness by Marpai’s CEO to support the company’s funding needs while deferring repayment, which may offer short-term financial flexibility but underscores the firm’s reliance on related-party debt. For stakeholders, the revised September 2026 maturities reduce immediate refinancing or repayment risk, potentially stabilizing the company’s cash planning over the next several months.

Spark’s Take on MRAI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MRAI is a Neutral.

The score is weighed down primarily by weak financial performance (shrinking revenue, large losses, negative equity, and ongoing cash burn) and a bearish technical trend (price below all major moving averages with negative MACD). Valuation offers limited support due to negative earnings and no dividend, while the earnings call/corporate financing actions show some operational progress but also reinforce near-term liquidity risk.

To see Spark’s full report on MRAI stock, click here.

More about Marpai

Marpai, Inc. operates in the health benefits and insurance administration sector, focusing on managing self-funded health plans for employers. The company leverages data and technology-driven solutions to optimize healthcare spending and improve plan performance for its clients in the U.S. employer-sponsored benefits market.

data.title: FirstCash Completes $750 Million Senior Notes Offering
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

FirstCash ( (FCFS) ) has issued an update.

On May 1, 2026, FirstCash Holdings, Inc. closed a privately placed $750 million offering of 6.125% senior unsecured notes due May 1, 2034, issued by its wholly owned subsidiary FirstCash, Inc. The notes, guaranteed by the parent and key domestic subsidiaries, carry semi-annual interest payments starting November 1, 2026, and were sold under Rule 144A and Regulation S to qualified investors.

The new notes include a detailed redemption schedule that allows early repayment with a make-whole premium before May 1, 2029 and step-down call prices thereafter, plus provisions for mandatory repurchase following certain asset sales or change-of-control events. Covenants in the indenture restrict additional indebtedness, dividends, stock repurchases, asset transfers, mergers, and affiliate transactions, signaling a disciplined capital structure framework designed to protect noteholders while providing FirstCash with long-term funding flexibility.

The most recent analyst rating on (FCFS) stock is a Buy with a $252.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on FirstCash stock, see the FCFS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FCFS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FCFS is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial quality (notably robust free cash flow and solid margins) and a clear upward technical trend. Offsetting factors are balance-sheet leverage volatility across periods and only moderate valuation support (P/E ~19.3 with a low dividend yield).

To see Spark’s full report on FCFS stock, click here.

More about FirstCash

FirstCash Holdings, Inc., through its subsidiary FirstCash, Inc., operates as a specialty finance company focused on pawn and related consumer financial services, primarily serving customers who lack access to traditional banking credit. The company typically funds its operations and growth through a mix of unsecured credit facilities and senior unsecured notes in the capital markets.

Average Trading Volume: 337,239

BioRestorative Therapies ( (BRTX) ) has issued an update.

On May 1, 2026, BioRestorative Therapies, Inc.’s compensation committee approved discretionary cash bonuses for its named executive officers for services rendered in 2025, reflecting increases over the prior year in both salary and equity-based awards. For 2025, Chief Executive Officer Lance Alstodt earned total compensation of about $2.41 million, VP of Research and Development Francisco Silva earned about $2.32 million, and Chief Financial Officer Robert Kristal earned about $0.80 million, signaling the board’s recognition of executive performance and ongoing investment in leadership.

The 2025 bonuses, to be paid in 2026, were granted on top of significant option awards valued under stock-based accounting rules, underscoring the company’s emphasis on equity incentives to align management with shareholder interests. Compared with 2024, the higher cash bonuses and option values for the top executives indicate a stronger compensation posture that may reflect the company’s assessment of progress in its operations and its desire to retain key talent in a competitive biotech market.
